hard.next_step: June 5 2026: questions received back Discovery calls to be initiated, with effort focused on engaging potential decision makers and broadening contacts beyond the single champion. Pricing strategy still being decided with internal teams. RFP response underway following the signed NDA, with the next stage being to influence the evaluation process and confirm the decision and sponsor roles.
hard.ais_why: Nutreco is running a global eSourcing standardization RFP: standardize sourcing workflow globally, improve efficiency and increase supplier competition and transparency, with policy/audit compliance and multi-geo scalability. The documented need is conventional eSourcing standardization with no AI requirement stated. It is an RFP-led evaluation currently single-threaded, with effort focused on broadening contacts beyond the single champion. Merlin Intake sits in scope but the requirement set is conventional eSourcing. Formal Evaluation, 20%.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live RFP response for a global eSourcing standardization at Nutreco, but single-threaded through one procurement evaluator (Letitia Mandache), no economic buyer or decision maker mapped, and the discovery calls that would open the account are planned but not yet booked. Last two-way contact was the 16-18 June email Q&A; pricing strategy was still being decided internally as of 5 June. Competitive shortlist in this multi-vendor RFP is unknown, and the buyer's documented need is conventional eSourcing (AIS Negative), so the Merlin/AI story is not landing.
north_star_verdict.math: As of 2026-07-06 the close date 2026-12-31 is roughly 178 days out. Opportunity created 2026-05-19 (age ~48 days), qualified 2026-05-22, moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 2026-05-29, so ~38 days in Formal Evaluation. Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-18 (18 days ago) was a two-way email Q&A exchange with Letitia Mandache. No close-date slip in field history (date stable). Formal Evaluation is the first active enterprise stage; in a 12-15 month new-logo cycle the deal is early but the close date is far enough out to be recoverable if discovery and multi-threading happen within 30 days. Verdict is Slowing, not Off Track: the buyer is still engaged (18-day-old two-way email), but the key advancing action (discovery calls to map criteria, decision process, and the economic buyer) is planned and not scheduled, and the deal is single-threaded at the procurement layer.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is documented but not quantified. Nutreco is running the RFP to standardize the sourcing workflow globally, improve efficiency, reduce manual effort, and increase supplier competition and transparency, with policy and audit compliance and multi-geo scalability (SF AIS_Why__c, 5 June 2026). These are stated objectives, not measured impact: there is no buyer-stated cost figure, savings target, cycle-time, headcount, or error-rate quantifying the pain. No Avoma discovery call exists to capture the numbers in the buyer's own words. The move is to use the planned discovery call with Letitia Mandache to quantify what manual, non-standard sourcing costs Nutreco today (spend under management, sourcing-cycle days, number of business units on disparate tools) so the proposal is anchored to their economics.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified in contact roles, fields, or any call. The two known contacts, Letitia Mandache (Procurement Excellence Manager, Evaluator) and Janneke Vollaers-Schepers (Global Procurement Manager Indirects), are procurement roles, not confirmed budget owners. The email trail is run entirely through Letitia as the RFP gatekeeper (SF Task, 16-18 June 2026), and the Next Step itself flags the gap: the next stage is to confirm the decision and sponsor roles (SF Next_Step__c, 5 June 2026). Nothing in the evidence names who signs off the spend. In a global $150k eSourcing standardization at a company the size of Nutreco, the economic buyer is likely a Group CPO or a finance sponsor above the procurement managers. The move is to ask Letitia directly, on the discovery call, who owns the budget and to request an introduction to the executive sponsor before commercials are submitted.

decision_criteria.narrative: The high-level criteria are visible from the RFP framing but the scored requirement detail is not yet in hand. Nutreco wants a platform that standardizes sourcing globally, drives efficiency and reduced manual effort, increases supplier competition and transparency, and meets policy and audit compliance with multi-geo scalability (SF AIS_Why__c, 5 June 2026). The evaluation is being run as a formal, transparency-controlled RFP: Letitia Mandache insisted that all clarification questions be submitted in writing to keep the transparency process as set out in the RFP documents (SF Task, 16 June 2026), which means scoring will follow the written RFP criteria rather than informal influence. The detailed weighting and must-have requirements have not yet been surfaced. The move is to obtain the RFP scoring criteria and evaluation stages on the discovery call so the response is written to the exact scored requirements.

decision_process.narrative: The process so far is a procurement-led, formal RFP: NDA signed and interest confirmed (SF Task, 27 May 2026), Nutreco's RFP issued, Zycus reviewing in detail and submitting written clarification questions, with Nutreco returning answers (SF Task, 16-18 June 2026). Letitia Mandache controls the process gate and enforces written, transparent Q&A rather than side conversations (SF Task, 16 June 2026), which signals a compliance-driven, auditable selection. The decision and sponsor roles are explicitly not yet locked (SF Next_Step__c, 5 June 2026), and no call sequence exists to read who escalates or joins later. The approval chain, number of evaluators, and the shortlist-to-award timeline are unknown. The move is to map the evaluation stages and the internal decision committee during discovery, because the process is being run at arm's length through a single procurement contact.

paper_process.narrative: The NDA is signed, which established the legal basis to exchange RFP information (SF Task, 19-27 May 2026). Beyond that, the contracting path is unmapped: no MSA, Order Form, or SOW is in play at Formal Evaluation, which is normal this early. As a global food and nutrition company headquartered in the Netherlands, Nutreco will require GDPR / data-processing terms and an InfoSec / security review before signature, and likely integration into their existing ERP and procurement systems, but none of these are yet scoped. As a Netherlands / Western-Europe buyer, a purchase order may not gate invoicing, so a missing PO later would not be a red flag. The move is to surface the contracting, legal, and InfoSec sequence on the discovery call so the path from proposal to signature by the 31 December close is visible.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is articulated at a policy level but not owned by a named person or quantified. Nutreco's current-state pain is fragmented, non-standard global sourcing with manual effort and limited supplier competition and transparency, plus policy and audit-compliance exposure across multiple geographies (SF AIS_Why__c, 5 June 2026). This is confirmed as the driver of the RFP, but it is inferred from the RFP framing rather than quoted from a buyer on a call, and no single pain owner (a procurement transformation lead or a business-unit sponsor feeling the cost) has been named. The move is to have Letitia Mandache name, on the discovery call, which business units and leaders feel this pain most acutely and what triggered the RFP now, to convert a documented objective into an owned, urgent pain.

champion.narrative: Letitia Mandache (Procurement Excellence Manager, Evaluator) is the single active contact and the RFP point of contact: she confirmed interest, is running the Q&A, and said she plans to answer Zycus's questions soonest (SF Task, 18 June 2026). She is engaged, but she is behaving as a neutral, process-enforcing gatekeeper rather than an internal advocate: she has insisted all contact stay inside the formal RFP transparency process (SF Task, 16 June 2026), and there is no evidence of her pushing Zycus internally, sharing competitive intel, or introducing an executive sponsor. What has developed: a responsive, cooperative primary contact. What is still missing: proof of advocacy and any access to the economic buyer or decision maker. The Next Step itself calls the deal single-threaded and targets broadening beyond the single champion (SF Next_Step__c, 5 June 2026). Champion strength is developing at best, and confirming whether Letitia can and will champion Zycus, or whether a parallel senior thread is needed, is the priority.

competition.narrative: The competitive shortlist is unknown. Nutreco is running an explicitly multi-vendor RFP (SF Task, 27 May 2026 and SF Next_Step__c, 5 June 2026), so Zycus is one of several vendors, but no competitor is named in any Salesforce field, task, next step, or call, and no incumbent sourcing tool is on record. There are no Avoma discovery calls to surface which rivals Nutreco is evaluating or what they favour. This is a genuine blind spot in a formal, scored RFP: without knowing the field, Zycus cannot counter-position or write differentiation into the response. The move is to extract, during discovery, who else is shortlisted, what Nutreco uses today, and what they like or dislike about the alternatives, so the RFP response is written against real rivals rather than in the dark. Per living memory no competitor has ever been named on this deal, so none is asserted here.

deal_movement.summary: A new-logo RFP that has moved from first contact to an active written evaluation in seven weeks, but is still single-threaded. NDA signed and interest confirmed in late May, stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 29 May, and a formal RFP Q&A exchange ran through 16-18 June. The last real two-way buyer touch was the 18 June email with Letitia Mandache; discovery calls and pricing remain internal-planning items, not yet executed with the buyer.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive field is unknown. Nutreco is running an explicitly multi-vendor RFP (SF Next_Step__c and RFP invitation, 27 May 2026), but no rival, incumbent, or current sourcing tool is named in any field, task, next step, or call, and no Avoma calls exist to surface the shortlist. Zycus is responding blind: no competitor can be ranked because none is evidenced. This is a knowledge gap to close on the discovery call, not a sole-source position. No competitor is asserted, consistent with living memory (none ever named on this deal).

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Nutreco's documented expectations are a conventional global eSourcing standardization: one standardized sourcing workflow across geographies, efficiency and reduced manual effort, more supplier competition and transparency, and policy/audit compliance with multi-geo scalability. iSource maps directly to the core eSourcing ask; the Merlin Intake / AI element is in scope but not part of the buyer's stated requirement (AIS Negative), so the AI story is exposed until Nutreco asks for it.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win levers on a single-threaded, blind RFP where the buyer's need is conventional eSourcing and our AI differentiation is not yet wanted.
flags: Multi-thread now: engage Janneke Vollaers-Schepers and secure an executive sponsor before RFP scoring locks, so we are not single-threaded through one evaluator.
Close the competitive blind spot: use discovery to name the shortlist and the incumbent tool so the RFP response can differentiate against real rivals.

Reframe value to the buyer's stated need: lead the response on global standardization, efficiency, and supplier transparency (iSource), and only layer Merlin Intake once AI appetite is confirmed (AIS Negative).

champion_strength.summary: Letitia Mandache is a responsive, cooperative RFP point of contact (Evaluator) but is acting as a neutral process gatekeeper, insisting all contact stay inside the formal transparency process (SF Task, 16 June 2026), with no evidence of internal advocacy or access to power. The Next Step itself flags the deal as single-threaded and targets broadening beyond the single champion (SF Next_Step__c, 5 June 2026). Developing at best: engagement is real, advocacy and executive access are unproven.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake is in the product scope but Nutreco's documented requirement is conventional eSourcing standardization with no AI requirement stated, so the AI/agentic value is under-positioned relative to what the buyer is actually asking for. Leading with AI now risks missing the RFP's scored criteria; the AI story should be introduced only after appetite is confirmed on discovery.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 3.0 Negative: the buyer's stated need is conventional global eSourcing standardization (efficiency, supplier transparency, compliance) with no AI requirement expressed (SF AIS_Why__c, 5 June 2026). Merlin Intake sits in scope but the requirement set is not AI-led, and no call exists to show latent AI appetite. Read as AI Resistant until discovery proves otherwise.

Pricing strategy still being decided internally as of 5 June and no buyer budget owner confirmed, risking a proposal that is not validated against the economic buyer's expectations.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A live but single-threaded RFP response at Nutreco: iSource fits the conventional global eSourcing need, but no economic buyer is mapped, the competitive field is unknown, and the whole deal runs through one procurement evaluator, so the win position is capped until discovery opens the account.

win_position.text: Zycus is one vendor in a multi-vendor RFP with no economic buyer mapped and the competitive shortlist unknown, so the position is unproven despite a live evaluation (SF Next_Step__c, 5 June 2026).
iSource maps directly to Nutreco's documented need to standardize sourcing globally and increase supplier competition and transparency (SF AIS_Why__c, 5 June 2026).
The buyer's need is conventional eSourcing with no AI requirement stated (AIS 3.0 Negative), so Merlin Intake and our AI differentiation are not currently wanted.
The deal is single-threaded through Letitia Mandache, who is enforcing arm's-length RFP transparency rather than championing Zycus (SF Task, 16 June 2026).

deal_momentum.text: The deal advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 29 May and ran an active written RFP Q&A with Nutreco through 18 June (SF field history; SF Task).
Momentum is stalling: discovery calls named in the 5 June Next Step are still not booked and the last two-way touch was the 18 June email.
No senior stakeholder has joined and the customer has not requested the next meeting; engagement is one procurement contact over email.

customer_commitment.text: Nutreco signed the NDA and formally confirmed Zycus's participation in the RFP (SF Task, 27 May 2026).
Commitment stops at process participation: no economic buyer, no decision date, and no Zycus-specific preference expressed by the buyer.
Letitia keeps all contact inside the formal transparency process, so there is no informal signal of a lean toward Zycus (SF Task, 16 June 2026).

deal_risk.text: No economic buyer or decision maker is mapped and the deal is single-threaded through one evaluator (SF Next_Step__c; SF OpportunityContactRole).
The competitive shortlist and incumbent tool are unknown in a formal multi-vendor RFP, so Zycus is responding blind (SF Task, 27 May 2026).
Pricing strategy was still being decided internally as of 5 June with no buyer budget owner confirmed, risking a misaligned proposal (SF Next_Step__c).

competitor_risk.evidence: Multi-vendor RFP with an unknown shortlist and incumbent; Zycus responding blind (SF Task, 27 May 2026).

economic_buyer.evidence: No economic buyer mapped; Next Step still targeting confirmation of decision and sponsor roles (SF Next_Step__c, 5 June 2026).

scope_change.detail: No scope change on record; product scope has been iSource plus Merlin Intake since creation, with no modules dropped or amount cut.

customer_preference.evidence: Neutral, formal RFP participation only; Letitia insists on written transparency-process Q&A and expresses no Zycus-specific lean (SF Task, 16 June 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative objectives (standardize sourcing globally, efficiency, supplier transparency, compliance) documented in the RFP but no cost, savings, or cycle-time quantified (SF AIS_Why__c, 5 June 2026).

critical_signals.text: This is a multi-vendor RFP but the shortlist and the tool Nutreco uses today are both unknown, so we are writing the response blind.
Janneke Vollaers-Schepers, Global Procurement Manager Indirects, is on the account but we have never engaged her, leaving us single-threaded through Letitia.
The last real exchange was the 18 June email Q&A where Letitia kept everything inside the formal RFP transparency process; no live call has yet happened.
Pricing strategy was still being decided internally as of 5 June and no budget owner is confirmed, so the proposal is not yet validated to a buyer.
Nutreco's documented need is conventional global eSourcing standardization with no AI requirement, so the Merlin Intake story is not landing.
